name: sidetone
description: Sidetone desktop app - WPF with C WASAPI audio engine for microphone sidetone through headphones
instructions: This is a WPF desktop app (.NET 5) with a C-based WASAPI audio engine. Key files: engine.c (audio processing), Native.cs (P/Invoke), MainWindow.xaml/cs (UI). The app provides microphone sidetone (hear your mic through headphones).

key_files: engine.c - C audio engine with WASAPI, Speex noise suppression, noise gate
app/Native.cs - P/Invoke declarations for engine functions
app/MainWindow.xaml - WPF UI with device dropdowns, sliders
context.vcpkg_note: build.bat auto-detects vcpkg from common locations - no manual path needed

known_behaviors: Bluetooth headphones have inherent Windows audio latency/distortion issues - app-level fixes limited
Sample rate mismatch between input/output devices can cause issues (display shows Hz)
Buffer size adjustment available in UI - higher values = more stable but more latency
Changing input/output devices while sidetone is running can cause issues - device dropdowns are now disabled while active

discovered_issues_and_solutions.issue: Warbly/distorted artifacts from noise suppression when not talking
attempts: Hard threshold gate with attack/release caused popping and distortion
Floor gain and margin gain multipliers caused more buzzing
discovered_issues_and_solutions.solution: Use Speex VAD combined with RMS threshold - only close gate when both VAD says no voice AND RMS below threshold

discovered_issues_and_solutions.issue: Gate chattering at syllable boundaries (rapid open/close)
discovered_issues_and_solutions.solution: Added hold counter (40 frames) so gate only closes after sustained no-voice, and slower attack (0.05) for smoother transitions

discovered_issues_and_solutions.issue: Tiny static/distortion for a couple ms when gate engages
discovered_issues_and_solutions.solution: Increased hold frames from 20 to 40, reduced attack from 0.1 to 0.05

discovered_issues_and_solutions.issue: Changing input/output devices while sidetone is running causes issues
discovered_issues_and_solutions.solution: Disable device dropdowns while sidetone is running, re-enable when stopped. Grey out dropdown text when disabled.

audio_pipeline.description: Audio signal flow from microphone to headphones
flow_diagram: -> Noise Gate (VAD + RMS hybrid with hold)

key_components.engine.c:main_loop: WASAPI capture/render loop, processes audio per-frame
key_components.engine.c:read_sample_as_float: Converts various sample formats (PCM16/32, float) to normalized float
key_components.engine.c:speex_preprocess_run: Runs Speex noise suppression and VAD on 256-sample frames
key_components.engine.c:gate_logic: VAD + RMS hybrid gate with 40-frame hold and smooth attack/release

audio_pipeline.timing_notes: FRAMES=32 (1ms at 32kHz), processing happens per-sample but Speex operates on 256-sample blocks

things_not_to_do: Do not touch or modify the Speex noise suppression logic - it is working perfectly
Do not bypass or remove the noise suppression - it is an important part of the audio pipeline
Do not use hard binary gate without hold counter - causes severe distortion
Do not use fast attack coefficients (> 0.1) - causes popping/pumping
always_run_build: After any code changes, always run build.bat in the app directory to verify the build succeeds before reporting completion
maintenance: Continuously update this skill file with important discoveries, solved problems, and dead-ends encountered during development to prevent future sessions from repeating the same mistakes

steps: 1. Ensure all changes are committed and pushed to master
2. Create a git tag with version number: git tag -a vX.Y.Z -m 'Release vX.Y.Z'
3. Push the tag: git push origin vX.Y.Z
4. Use gh release create to create release with changelog
5. Attach the portable app from app/publish folder
github_release_instructions.important_note: ALWAYS wait for user confirmation that fixes/features work before doing any GitHub push. Test the build locally first and get user approval before committing/pushing.
github_release_instructions.example_release: gh release create v1.0.0 --title 'Sidetone v1.0.0' --notes-file CHANGELOG.md
